Private functions for initializing and configuring RTC drivers on STM32 platforms.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file named `rtc_private.h` that declares functions used internally by RTC (Real-Time Clock) driver implementations for STM32 platforms. The functions are intended for configuring and verifying the clock source, specifically the LSE (Low-Speed External) oscillator, which is part of the RCC (Reset and Clock Control) system. The function [`rtc_init_config_clock_source`](<#rtc_init_config_clock_source>) initializes the clock source and returns a boolean indicating success. The function [`rtc_init_config_lse_clock_source`](<#rtc_init_config_lse_clock_source>) configures the LSE oscillator, while [`rtc_init_verify_clock_source_config`](<#rtc_init_verify_clock_source_config>) checks the correctness of the clock source setup. Additionally, [`rtc_enable_backup_regs`](<#rtc_enable_backup_regs>) enables access to the RTC's backup registers. These functions are marked as private, indicating they are not intended for use outside the RTC driver implementations.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
